By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō Era|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|12=draft|12=draft}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Jinmei Era|ja|貞応|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=draft|12=draft}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|12=draft|12=draft}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|12=draft|12=draft}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namesa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō Era|ja|享保}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}}. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村}} in {{ill|Jinmei Era|ja|貞応}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namesa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Namesa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:namesajinjahonden.JPG|180px|Main hall of Namesa Shrine]]
Main hall | location = {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県出雲市東神西町|qq=draft}} 720 | coordinates = {{ill|35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|ja}}|deity = {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft}})
{{ill|Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō Era|ja|享保|qq=draft}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}}. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Jinmei Era|ja|貞応|qq=draft}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namesa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō Era|ja|享保|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō Era|qq=}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in {{ill|Jinmei Era|ja|貞応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmei Era|qq=}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namesa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Namesa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:namesajinjahonden.JPG|180px|Main hall of Namesa Shrine]]
Main hall | location = {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=}} 720 | coordinates = {{ill|35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|qq=}}|deity = {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}})
{{ill|Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|qq=}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō Era|ja|享保|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō Era|qq=}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in {{ill|Jinmei Era|ja|貞応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmei Era|qq=}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Namesa Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|qq=}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō Era|ja|享保|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō Era|qq=}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in {{ill|Jinmei Era|ja|貞応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmei Era|qq=}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji Era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|ja|本居宣長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoori Norinaga|qq=}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Namesa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:namesajinjahonden.JPG|180px|Main hall of Namesa Shrine]]
Main hall | location = {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=}} 720 | coordinates = {{ill|35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|qq=}}|deity = {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]])
{{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|qq=}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō Era]] 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]]

Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]] [[Category:出雲市の建築物]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]]

{{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|qq=}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō Era]] 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō Era]] 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[en:Namesa Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:出雲市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|qq=}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō Era]] 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo City]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Go-sha]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

Main hall | location = {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 720 | coordinates = {{ill|35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|deity = {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]])
{{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|qq=}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō Era]] 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:History of Izumo City]] [[Category:run it on these again]][[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō Era]] 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}" to "|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Namesa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:namesajinjahonden.JPG|180px|Main hall of Namesa Shrine]]
Main hall | location = {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 720 | coordinates = {{ill|35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/35°18′51″N 132°42′0″E|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|deity = {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]])
{{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|qq=}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]]) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|qq=}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|qq=}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō Era]] 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]]. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|qq=}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|qq=}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|qq=}} in [[:en:Jōō (Kamakura period)|Jinmei Era]] 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == [[:en:Motoori Norinaga|Motoori Norinaga]] proposed that the shrine mentioned in [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[sn:Namesa Shrine]][Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Namesa Shrine]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]
