{{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|1=Ōtori-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441991}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|1=Jinzai Ko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590815}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō Era|1=Kyōhō}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|1=Jinzaimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105619114}} in {{ill|Jōō (Kamakura period)|en|Jōō (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1194163|lt=Jinmei Era|1=Jōō (Kamakura period)}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Namesa Shrine (Q11642725)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kando district|ja|神門郡|zh|神門郡|WD=Q11591020|1=Kando district}} || {{ill|Namesa Shrine|lt=Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|WD=Q11642725|1=Namesa Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.314364|132.699845|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182917 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11642725}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q11642725]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Namesa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Namesajinjahonden.JPG | location = {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県出雲市東神西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 720 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.314167|N|132.7|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity = {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}})
{{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|1=Ōtori-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441991}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|1=Jinzai Ko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590815}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō Era|1=Kyōhō}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|1=Jinzaimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105619114}} in {{ill|Jōō (Kamakura period)|en|Jōō (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1194163|lt=Jinmei Era|1=Jōō (Kamakura period)}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Namesa Shrine (Q11642725)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kando district|ja|神門郡|zh|神門郡|WD=Q11591020|1=Kando district}} || {{ill|Namesa Shrine|lt=Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|WD=Q11642725|1=Namesa Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.314364|132.699845|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182917 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11642725}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q11642725]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|1=Jinzai Ko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590815}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō Era|1=Kyōhō}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|1=Jinzaimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105619114}} in {{ill|Jōō (Kamakura period)|en|Jōō (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1194163|lt=Jinmei Era|1=Jōō (Kamakura period)}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Namesa Shrine (Q11642725)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Namesa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Namesajinjahonden.JPG | location = {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県出雲市東神西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 720 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.314167|N|132.7|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity = {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}})
{{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suserihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Ōtori-zukuri|ja|大鳥造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtori-zukuri|1=Ōtori-zukuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11441991}} | Other names = Takakura Myōjin | Main Festival = April 23 | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} }} '''Namesa Shrine''' (那売佐神社, なめさじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja|出雲市東神西町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Jinsei Town, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} with the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. The shrine is situated at the foot of Takakura Mountain, southeast of {{ill|Lake Jinsei|ja|神西湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Jinsei|1=Jinzai Ko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590815}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Ashihara no Shikoo|ja|葦原醜男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashihara no Shikoo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (an epithet of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}) * {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} == History == The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} under the {{ill|Kamimiguni District|ja|神門郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimiguni District|1=Kando district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591020}} entry as "Nasa Shrine" (奈賣佐社) and "Namesa Shrine" (那賣佐社). By the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} period, the shrine had already merged two Namesa Shrines into one. In {{ill|Tenna Era|ja|天和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenna Era|1=Tenna (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442381}} 3 (1683), the {{ill|Izumo Fudokishō|ja|出雲風土記鈔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudokishō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} refers to the shrine as "Iwatsubo Myōjin," and by {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō Era|1=Kyōhō}} 2 (1717), it was called "Takakura Myōjin." Records suggest the shrine was relocated from its original site, Iwatsubo, to the current location during the late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Grounds == * Haiden (worship hall): Irimoya-zukuri, tiled roof * Honden (main hall): Modified Taisha-zukuri, bark thatched roof * Izanagi Shrine A trail starting from the shrine grounds leads to the summit of {{ill|Mount Jinsei|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Jinsei|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}, the site of the historical {{ill|Jinsei Castle|ja|神西城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Castle|1=Q11590792|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590792}}. == Sub-Shrines == ;Jinsei Hachiman Shrine : Located in Higashi Jinsei Town Nakagumi, it was originally enshrined in {{ill|Jinsei Village|ja|神西村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinsei Village|1=Jinzaimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105619114}} in {{ill|Jōō (Kamakura period)|en|Jōō (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1194163|lt=Jinmei Era|1=Jōō (Kamakura period)}} 2 (1223) by the local steward Ono Takamichi. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was merged with Namesa Shrine along with its subsidiary shrines. ;Kifune Shrine : Located in two locations in Higashi Jinsei Town: Takaiwa and Inochi. Both were merged with Namesa Shrine in 1910. == Iwatsubo == [[File:Izumoiwatsubo.JPG|thumb|Iwatsubo]] The {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} states that {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} visited {{ill|Suseri-Hime|ja|須勢理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseri-Hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}} at this site and remarked on a smooth rock, naming it "nameru Iwa" (滑磐石). This rock, identified as the pothole "Iwatsubo" in the nearby {{ill|Kukegawa River|ja|九景川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukegawa River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is considered the origin of the local area's name. == Alternative Theories == {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|WD=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}} proposed that the shrine mentioned in {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} as "Iwakuma no Sono Miya" (石くま之曽宮) might be Namesa Shrine instead of the nearby [[Izumo-taisha]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Shikinaisha Research Society, "Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 21: Izumo (B), Iwami, Oki," 皇學館大学出版部, 1983. * Sekikazu, "Izumo Fudoki Annotated Commentary," 明石書店, 2006, ISBN 4-7503-2376-4. * Hamamura Daijirō, "Revised History of Jinsei Village," Jinsei Public Hall, 1988 (original publication: 1934). {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|那売佐神社|version=85436980|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Namesa Shrine (Q11642725)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumo Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kando district|ja|神門郡|zh|神門郡|WD=Q11591020|1=Kando district}} || {{ill|Namesa Shrine|lt=Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|WD=Q11642725|1=Namesa Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.314364|132.699845|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182917 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11642725}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q11642725]] [[ja:那売佐神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kando district|ja|神門郡|zh|神門郡|WD=Q11591020|1=Kando district}} || {{ill|Namesa Shrine|lt=Namesa Shrine|ja|那売佐神社|WD=Q11642725|1=Namesa Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.314364|132.699845|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182917 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11642725|da|d:Q11642725|ja|那売佐神社}}==Notes== [[Category:Buildings and structures in Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Izumo, Shimane]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
